source.title: Optimization Suggestions (Console) — Events Taxonomy v0 (beta funnel + diagnostics)
source.content:
Context
- Feature: Redwood Console > Optimize > Suggestions (beta) for batching/caching recommendations.
- Goal: closed-loop measurement of usefulness + trust: impressions → expand → dismiss → apply click. Also capture basic diagnostics for empty/error states without collecting customer content.
- This doc proposes a v0 event taxonomy aligned to the funnel dashboard spec and the Suggestions API contract (stable suggestion IDs).

Principles / requirements
1) No customer content
- Do NOT include: prompt text, completion text, request/response bodies, raw config payloads, or any user-entered notes.
- Do NOT include: IP address, email, name, or free-form text fields.
- Route identifiers: use Redwood route_id and/or route_name if already considered non-sensitive within the org boundary; if route_name may contain customer data, prefer route_id only.

2) Stable join keys for analysis
- suggestion_id must be stable across recomputations per ADR.
- Include org_id + project_id for scoping.
- Include model_id and route_id for drilldowns.

3) Event minimalism + versioning
- Every event must include event_version and schema_version (string) to support forwards-compatible evolution.
- Use consistent naming and property keys across UI + API surfaces.

4) Dedupe and counting rules
- Impressions should be deduped per (viewer_session_id, suggestion_id) within a short TTL (recommend 30 minutes) to avoid inflation from re-renders.
- Expand is counted on explicit user action (row click / “View details”), not on auto-open.
- Dismiss is counted when user explicitly dismisses a suggestion in UI (not when it disappears due to recompute).
- Apply-click is a CTA click that deep-links to the relevant config page. We explicitly do NOT claim settings were actually applied.

5) Beta gating
- Include optimize_suggestions_beta_enabled boolean (from feature flag) and cohort label where available.

Event transport
- Client-side events (Console web): funnel interactions + UI state.
- Server-side events (Suggestions API): endpoint performance + result sizes, used for operational dashboards and to reconcile funnel anomalies.

Common fields (required on all events)
- event_name: string
- event_version: string (e.g., "v0")
- schema_version: string (e.g., "2026-02-15")
- timestamp: ISO-8601 (generated by client/server)
- org_id: string
- project_id: string
- environment: string ("prod" | "staging" | "dev")
- region: string (Console region or API region; if unknown set "unknown")
- actor_type: string ("human")
- viewer_user_id_hash: string (one-way hash; stable per user; never raw user id/email)
- viewer_session_id: string (random per session)
- optimize_suggestions_beta_enabled: string ("true" | "false")

Context fields (optional but recommended)
- page_path: string (e.g., "/console/optimize/suggestions")
- referrer_path: string
- ui_surface: string ("console")
- ui_build_sha: string
- ui_release_channel: string ("beta" | "stable")
- org_plan_tier: string ("hosted" | "dedicated" | "private" | "unknown")

Suggestion identity fields (required for suggestion-level events)
- suggestion_id: string (stable id)
- suggestion_type: string ("batching" | "caching")
- route_id: string
- model_id: string
- suggestion_status: string ("active" | "stale" | "expired" | "unknown")
- stale_as_of: string (ISO-8601 or empty if not provided)

Risk/estimate summary fields (optional; include only what is already shown in UI)
- risk_level: string ("low" | "medium" | "high" | "unknown")
- confidence_level: string ("low" | "medium" | "high" | "unknown")
- est_savings_usd_per_day: string (rounded, e.g., "125.50"; allow empty)
- est_savings_pct: string (rounded percent string, e.g., "12.3"; allow empty)
- est_latency_p50_delta_ms: string (rounded, allow empty)
- est_latency_p95_delta_ms: string (rounded, allow empty)

NOTE: values are strings to satisfy downstream ingestion constraints; numeric parsing happens in the analytics pipeline.

----------------------------------------
Client-side (Console) events

1) optimize_suggestions_list_impression
When to fire
- Once when the Suggestions list/table first renders with N>0 items.
- Fire again only if filters are changed and a materially different list is rendered (see list_context_hash below).

Required fields
- list_context_hash: string (hash of filter+sort+time range state)
- suggestions_count: string

Recommended fields
- sort_key: string ("savings" | "risk" | "confidence" | "latency_impact" | "unknown")
- sort_order: string ("asc" | "desc")
- filter_risk_levels: string (comma-separated values)
- filter_suggestion_types: string (comma-separated values)
- filter_models: string (comma-separated model_ids or "all")
- filter_routes: string (comma-separated route_ids or "all")

Notes
- Do not attach per-suggestion payload to the list impression event (avoid large payloads + privacy risk). Item-level interactions cover suggestion_id.

2) optimize_suggestions_row_impression
When to fire
- (Optional, v0.1) If we need item-level viewability; otherwise skip for beta to limit volume.

If enabled, required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- position_index: string (0-based)
- viewport_state: string ("in_view" | "above_fold" | "below_fold")

3) optimize_suggestions_details_expand
When to fire
- User opens the details drawer for a specific suggestion.

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- expand_source: string ("row_click" | "view_details_button" | "deep_link" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- position_index: string
- list_context_hash: string
- risk_level, confidence_level (if shown on the row)

4) optimize_suggestions_details_close
When to fire
- User closes details drawer.

Required fields
- suggestion_id

Recommended fields
- close_reason: string ("x_button" | "esc" | "click_outside" | "navigate" | "unknown")
- dwell_time_ms: string (rounded)

5) optimize_suggestions_dismiss
When to fire
- User clicks “Dismiss” on a suggestion (row or drawer).

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields

Recommended fields
- dismiss_location: string ("row" | "drawer")
- dismiss_reason: string (enum only; NO free text)

Dismiss reason enum (v0)
- "not_relevant"
- "already_configured"
- "risk_too_high"
- "estimate_not_trusted"
- "just_browsing"
- "other" (still no free text)

6) optimize_suggestions_apply_click
When to fire
- User clicks the CTA to apply/enable the recommendation (deep link to config or rollout flow).

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- apply_target: string ("batching_config" | "caching_config" | "rollout_policy" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- apply_location: string ("row" | "drawer")
- destination_path: string (Console internal path only; strip query parameters if they can contain sensitive values)

Important
- This event measures intent and navigation, not actual config change.

7) optimize_suggestions_filters_change
When to fire
- User changes any filter chip or the sort order.

Required fields
- list_context_hash_before: string
- list_context_hash_after: string

Recommended fields
- changed_field: string ("risk" | "type" | "model" | "route" | "sort" | "search" | "unknown")

8) optimize_suggestions_empty_state_view
When to fire
- Suggestions page renders an empty state.

Required fields
- empty_state_type: string

Empty state type enum (v0)
- "no_suggestions"
- "insufficient_telemetry"
- "feature_not_enabled"
- "rbac_denied"
- "api_error"

Recommended fields
- api_error_code: string (if available)
- compute_staleness_hours: string (if shown/available)

9) optimize_suggestions_help_link_click
When to fire
- User clicks “How is this computed?” / “Learn more” / risk tooltip docs link.

Required fields
- help_topic: string ("savings_estimation" | "risk_levels" | "telemetry_requirements" | "batching" | "caching" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- link_destination: string ("docs" | "confluence" | "external")

----------------------------------------
Server-side (API) events

10) optimize_suggestions_api_list_request
When to fire
- Suggestions list endpoint is called (server emits once per request).

Required fields
- request_id: string
- org_id, project_id
- http_method: string ("GET")
- http_route: string ("/optimize/suggestions")
- http_status: string
- latency_ms: string (p50/p95 computed in metrics; still include per-event for sampling-based analysis)

Recommended fields
- page_size: string
- returned_count: string
- sort_key, sort_order
- filter_risk_levels, filter_suggestion_types
- cache_hit: string ("true" | "false" | "unknown") (server-side cache, not model KV cache)

11) optimize_suggestions_api_details_request
When to fire
- Suggestion details endpoint is called (if separate).

Required fields
- request_id
- suggestion_id
- http_status
- latency_ms

----------------------------------------
Privacy / security notes
- Pseudonymous user id: viewer_user_id_hash must be generated via approved hashing strategy (Security review: Marcus Lin). No raw user identifiers.
- Do not log any request/response bodies; ensure structured logging filters drop known sensitive keys.
- destination_path must not include query params that can contain customer identifiers; prefer routing name + route_id.
- For EU orgs, confirm that analytics processing falls under existing DPA; if not, gate event emission behind telemetry consent flag.

Operational notes (volume + sampling)
- List impression + expand + dismiss + apply-click are low/medium volume and should be unsampled in beta.
- API request events may be sampled (e.g., 10%) if needed; metrics (RED) cover core operational monitoring.

Mapping to dashboards (Suggestions beta funnel)
Primary funnel panel
- Impressions: count(optimize_suggestions_list_impression)
- Expanded: count(optimize_suggestions_details_expand)
- Dismissed: count(optimize_suggestions_dismiss)
- Apply intent: count(optimize_suggestions_apply_click)

Recommended breakdown dimensions
- org_id (top N), project_id
- suggestion_type
- risk_level, confidence_level
- model_id, route_id
- region
- ui_release_channel

Quality/trust diagnostics panels
- Dismiss reason distribution: group by dismiss_reason
- Expand → apply click conversion by risk_level/confidence_level
- Empty state rates: count(optimize_suggestions_empty_state_view) by empty_state_type

API health panels (operational)
- RPS: count(optimize_suggestions_api_list_request)
- Error rate: % http_status >= 500
- Latency: p50/p95 from metrics; supplement with sampled latency_ms
- Returned count distribution (detect sudden drop to 0)

Open questions / TODO
- Do we need optimize_suggestions_row_impression for item-level viewability? (Likely defer until post-beta to reduce noise.)
- Standardize route_name usage: confirm whether route_name is always customer-safe; otherwise drop and use route_id only.
- Confirm if we need a dedicated "suggestion_seen_at" server field vs analytics event; current plan is analytics only.

Implementation checklist (v0)
- [ ] Console emits: list_impression, details_expand/close, dismiss, apply_click, empty_state_view, help_link_click
- [ ] API emits: list_request (+ details_request if applicable)
- [ ] Add event_version/schema_version constants to both FE and BE
- [ ] Add dedupe for list_impression per session + list_context_hash
- [ ] Validate events do not include disallowed fields (prompt, completion, headers, IP)

Example (pseudo-payload, for reference only)
Event: optimize_suggestions_apply_click
{
  event_name: "optimize_suggestions_apply_click",
  event_version: "v0",
  schema_version: "2026-02-15",
  org_id: "org_123",
  project_id: "proj_abc",
  viewer_user_id_hash: "u_8f3c…",
  viewer_session_id: "sess_4d1…",
  suggestion_id: "sugg_01J2…",
  suggestion_type: "batching",
  route_id: "route_9d2…",
  model_id: "llama-3.1-70b-instruct",
  risk_level: "medium",
  confidence_level: "high",
  est_savings_usd_per_day: "125.50",
  est_latency_p95_delta_ms: "18",
  apply_target: "batching_config",
  apply_location: "drawer",
  destination_path: "/console/projects/proj_abc/routes/route_9d2/config/batching"
}

- org_plan_tier: string ("hosted" | "dedicated" | "private" | "unknown")

Suggestion identity fields (required for suggestion-level events)
- suggestion_id: string (stable id)
- suggestion_type: string ("batching" | "caching")
- route_id: string
- model_id: string
- suggestion_status: string ("active" | "stale" | "expired" | "unknown")
- stale_as_of: string (ISO-8601 or empty if not provided)

Risk/estimate summary fields (optional; include only what is already shown in UI)
- risk_level: string ("low" | "medium" | "high" | "unknown")
- confidence_level: string ("low" | "medium" | "high" | "unknown")
- est_savings_usd_per_day: string (rounded, e.g., "125.50"; allow empty)
- est_savings_pct: string (rounded percent string, e.g., "12.3"; allow empty)
- est_latency_p50_delta_ms: string (rounded, allow empty)
- est_latency_p95_delta_ms: string (rounded, allow empty)

NOTE: values are strings to satisfy downstream ingestion constraints; numeric parsing happens in the analytics pipeline.

----------------------------------------
Client-side (Console) events

1) optimize_suggestions_list_impression
When to fire
- Once when the Suggestions list/table first renders with N>0 items.
- Fire again only if filters are changed and a materially different list is rendered (see list_context_hash below).

Required fields
- list_context_hash: string (hash of filter+sort+time range state)
- suggestions_count: string

Recommended fields
- sort_key: string ("savings" | "risk" | "confidence" | "latency_impact" | "unknown")
- sort_order: string ("asc" | "desc")
- filter_risk_levels: string (comma-separated values)
- filter_suggestion_types: string (comma-separated values)
- filter_models: string (comma-separated model_ids or "all")
- filter_routes: string (comma-separated route_ids or "all")

Notes
- Do not attach per-suggestion payload to the list impression event (avoid large payloads + privacy risk). Item-level interactions cover suggestion_id.

2) optimize_suggestions_row_impression
When to fire
- (Optional, v0.1) If we need item-level viewability; otherwise skip for beta to limit volume.

If enabled, required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- position_index: string (0-based)
- viewport_state: string ("in_view" | "above_fold" | "below_fold")

3) optimize_suggestions_details_expand
When to fire
- User opens the details drawer for a specific suggestion.

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- expand_source: string ("row_click" | "view_details_button" | "deep_link" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- position_index: string
- list_context_hash: string
- risk_level, confidence_level (if shown on the row)

4) optimize_suggestions_details_close
When to fire
- User closes details drawer.

Required fields
- suggestion_id

Recommended fields
- close_reason: string ("x_button" | "esc" | "click_outside" | "navigate" | "unknown")
- dwell_time_ms: string (rounded)

5) optimize_suggestions_dismiss
When to fire
- User clicks “Dismiss” on a suggestion (row or drawer).

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields

Recommended fields
- dismiss_location: string ("row" | "drawer")
- dismiss_reason: string (enum only; NO free text)

Dismiss reason enum (v0)
- "not_relevant"
- "already_configured"
- "risk_too_high"
- "estimate_not_trusted"
- "just_browsing"
- "other" (still no free text)

6) optimize_suggestions_apply_click
When to fire
- User clicks the CTA to apply/enable the recommendation (deep link to config or rollout flow).

Required fields
- suggestion_id + suggestion identity fields
- apply_target: string ("batching_config" | "caching_config" | "rollout_policy" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- apply_location: string ("row" | "drawer")
- destination_path: string (Console internal path only; strip query parameters if they can contain sensitive values)

Important
- This event measures intent and navigation, not actual config change.

7) optimize_suggestions_filters_change
When to fire
- User changes any filter chip or the sort order.

Required fields
- list_context_hash_before: string
- list_context_hash_after: string

Recommended fields
- changed_field: string ("risk" | "type" | "model" | "route" | "sort" | "search" | "unknown")

8) optimize_suggestions_empty_state_view
When to fire
- Suggestions page renders an empty state.

Required fields
- empty_state_type: string

Empty state type enum (v0)
- "no_suggestions"
- "insufficient_telemetry"
- "feature_not_enabled"
- "rbac_denied"
- "api_error"

Recommended fields
- api_error_code: string (if available)
- compute_staleness_hours: string (if shown/available)

9) optimize_suggestions_help_link_click
When to fire
- User clicks “How is this computed?” / “Learn more” / risk tooltip docs link.

Required fields
- help_topic: string ("savings_estimation" | "risk_levels" | "telemetry_requirements" | "batching" | "caching" | "unknown")

Recommended fields
- link_destination: string ("docs" | "confluence" | "external")

----------------------------------------
Server-side (API) events

10) optimize_suggestions_api_list_request
When to fire
- Suggestions list endpoint is called (server emits once per request).

Required fields
- request_id: string
- org_id, project_id
- http_method: string ("GET")
- http_route: string ("/optimize/suggestions")
- http_status: string
- latency_ms: string (p50/p95 computed in metrics; still include per-event for sampling-based analysis)

Recommended fields
- page_size: string
- returned_count: string
- sort_key, sort_order
- filter_risk_levels, filter_suggestion_types
- cache_hit: string ("true" | "false" | "unknown") (server-side cache, not model KV cache)

11) optimize_suggestions_api_details_request
When to fire
- Suggestion details endpoint is called (if separate).

Required fields
- request_id
- suggestion_id
- http_status
- latency_ms

----------------------------------------
Privacy / security notes
- Pseudonymous user id: viewer_user_id_hash must be generated via approved hashing strategy (Security review: Marcus Lin). No raw user identifiers.
- Do not log any request/response bodies; ensure structured logging filters drop known sensitive keys.
- destination_path must not include query params that can contain customer identifiers; prefer routing name + route_id.
- For EU orgs, confirm that analytics processing falls under existing DPA; if not, gate event emission behind telemetry consent flag.

Operational notes (volume + sampling)
- List impression + expand + dismiss + apply-click are low/medium volume and should be unsampled in beta.
- API request events may be sampled (e.g., 10%) if needed; metrics (RED) cover core operational monitoring.

Mapping to dashboards (Suggestions beta funnel)
Primary funnel panel
- Impressions: count(optimize_suggestions_list_impression)
- Expanded: count(optimize_suggestions_details_expand)
- Dismissed: count(optimize_suggestions_dismiss)
- Apply intent: count(optimize_suggestions_apply_click)

Recommended breakdown dimensions
- org_id (top N), project_id
- suggestion_type
- risk_level, confidence_level
- model_id, route_id
- region
- ui_release_channel

Quality/trust diagnostics panels
- Dismiss reason distribution: group by dismiss_reason
- Expand → apply click conversion by risk_level/confidence_level
- Empty state rates: count(optimize_suggestions_empty_state_view) by empty_state_type

API health panels (operational)
- RPS: count(optimize_suggestions_api_list_request)
- Error rate: % http_status >= 500
- Latency: p50/p95 from metrics; supplement with sampled latency_ms
- Returned count distribution (detect sudden drop to 0)

Open questions / TODO
- Do we need optimize_suggestions_row_impression for item-level viewability? (Likely defer until post-beta to reduce noise.)
- Standardize route_name usage: confirm whether route_name is always customer-safe; otherwise drop and use route_id only.
- Confirm if we need a dedicated "suggestion_seen_at" server field vs analytics event; current plan is analytics only.

Implementation checklist (v0)
- [ ] Console emits: list_impression, details_expand/close, dismiss, apply_click, empty_state_view, help_link_click
- [ ] API emits: list_request (+ details_request if applicable)
- [ ] Add event_version/schema_version constants to both FE and BE
- [ ] Add dedupe for list_impression per session + list_context_hash
- [ ] Validate events do not include disallowed fields (prompt, completion, headers, IP)

Example (pseudo-payload, for reference only)
Event: optimize_suggestions_apply_click
{
  event_name: "optimize_suggestions_apply_click",
  event_version: "v0",
  schema_version: "2026-02-15",
  org_id: "org_123",
  project_id: "proj_abc",
  viewer_user_id_hash: "u_8f3c…",
  viewer_session_id: "sess_4d1…",
  suggestion_id: "sugg_01J2…",
  suggestion_type: "batching",
  route_id: "route_9d2…",
  model_id: "llama-3.1-70b-instruct",
  risk_level: "medium",
  confidence_level: "high",
  est_savings_usd_per_day: "125.50",
  est_latency_p95_delta_ms: "18",
  apply_target: "batching_config",
  apply_location: "drawer",
  destination_path: "/console/projects/proj_abc/routes/route_9d2/config/batching"
}

source.title: Suggestions API performance budget (beta) — latency, payload, rate limits, caching
source.content:
Doc intent
- Capture performance + scale budgets for the Console “Optimization suggestions” beta API.
- This is a constraints doc for engineering: if we can’t meet these numbers, Console UX (table + drawer) will feel sluggish and we’ll get pageload regressions.

Scope
- Suggestions list endpoint + (optional) details endpoint.
- Beta scope = batching + caching suggestions only.
- Org/project scoped, RBAC enforced. No cross-tenant access.

Non-goals
- Real-time per-request inference; suggestions are batch computed and served from storage.
- Guaranteeing “savings accuracy” (handled separately); this doc is about runtime performance and availability.

Assumptions / traffic model (beta)
- Primary UI path: Console loads Suggestions view once per user session; user may sort/filter/paginate; opens 0–5 drawers.
- Expected “active beta orgs”: 10–30 orgs initially, scaling to 100–200 orgs during expanded beta.
- Concurrent interactive users: 1–10 per org during working hours (spiky), with occasional demos.
- Background job compute cadence: hourly default (can be daily if compute pressure), but serving layer must tolerate stale data.

API endpoints (working contract)
- GET /optimize/suggestions
  - Query params: org_id, project_id (optional), environment (optional), route_id (optional), model (optional), region (optional), suggestion_type (batching|caching), sort, order, page_size, cursor
  - Returns: list of suggestion summaries, next_cursor, stale_as_of, request_id
- GET /optimize/suggestions/{suggestion_id}
  - Returns: the full details payload used by the drawer (why/explanation, assumptions, guardrails, links)

NOTE: If details endpoint is too expensive, we can embed “drawer-lite” content in list response for top N, but that likely increases payload size and hurts perceived speed on the main view.

Performance targets (server-side, excluding client rendering)
Table legend:
- p50/p95 measured at API gateway for 200 responses
- payload size is gzip’ed (over-the-wire) unless stated otherwise
- “cold” = cache miss at service cache layer; “warm” = cache hit

Table: latency + payload budgets
| Surface | Scenario | Target p50 | Target p95 | Hard ceiling p95 | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| GET /optimize/suggestions (list) | warm cache | 80 ms | 180 ms | 300 ms | Primary table load + filter/sort changes |
| GET /optimize/suggestions (list) | cold cache | 140 ms | 300 ms | 450 ms | Cold allowed but should be rare in interactive paths |
| GET /optimize/suggestions/{id} (details) | warm cache | 60 ms | 150 ms | 250 ms | Drawer open should feel instant |
| GET /optimize/suggestions/{id} (details) | cold cache | 120 ms | 250 ms | 400 ms | Drawer tolerates slightly higher; still keep tight |

Table: payload budgets
| Response | Target (gzip) | Hard ceiling (gzip) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| list page (<= 50 rows) | <= 35 KB | 60 KB | Prefer page_size default 25; 50 max |
| details payload | <= 20 KB | 40 KB | Avoid giant “explanation” blobs; link to docs when needed |
| error payload | <= 2 KB | 5 KB | Keep errors structured, don’t dump stack traces |

Page size / pagination budget
- Default page_size: 25
- Max page_size: 50 (server-enforced)
- Cursor-based pagination required for stable ordering + performance at scale.
- Sorting should be backed by indexed columns; forbid unindexed sorts.

Rate limits (beta)
Rationale: prevent accidental UI loops / eager refresh from hammering backend; keep headroom for compute pipeline + other Optimize traffic.

Table: rate limits
| Principal | Endpoint | Limit | Burst | Window | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| per-user | list | 60 req/min | 20 | 60s | Filter/sort + pagination should be within this |
| per-user | details | 120 req/min | 40 | 60s | Drawer opens can be chatty; still bounded |
| per-org | list | 600 req/min | 200 | 60s | Demos + multiple users |
| per-org | details | 1200 req/min | 400 | 60s | |
| per-IP (fallback) | all | 3000 req/5min | 500 | 300s | DDoS-ish guardrail |

Client guidance to reduce request volume
- UI should debounce filter changes (>= 250–400ms).
- Use “Apply filters” button if we see over-fetching.
- Cache list pages client-side for 30–60s keyed by (org, project, filters, sort, cursor).

Caching strategy
Goals:
- Keep list endpoint p95 < 300ms reliably.
- Avoid repeated DB hits for identical list queries during active usage.
- Support multi-tenant correctness (cache keys must include org_id + project scope + RBAC context).

Proposed layers
1) CDN / edge caching: NOT recommended for beta
- Reason: RBAC + org scoped data; cache fragmentation and security risk.

2) Service-level cache (recommended)
- Cache store: Redis (or equivalent) in-region with the service.
- Cache key inputs (must include): org_id, project_id (or “all”), environment, route_id, model, region, suggestion_type, sort, order, cursor/page boundary, page_size, and a “rbac_scope_hash”.
- TTL:
  - list pages: 60s default (tunable 30–180s)
  - details: 5m default (tunable 2m–15m)
- Invalidation:
  - Passive TTL is fine for beta.
  - Optional: invalidate org keys when new suggestion batch is published (publish event emits org_id + generation timestamp).

3) DB query caching (not primary)
- Avoid relying on DB-level cache; we want deterministic performance with predictable memory pressure.

Staleness behavior
- API must return: stale_as_of timestamp for the dataset used to generate suggestions.
- Console should show “Updated X hours ago” and avoid auto-refresh loops.
- If stale_as_of > 48h (configurable), show warning state but still serve data.

DB / storage performance requirements
- The list endpoint must be implementable as:
  - 1 query for suggestions page (plus possibly 1 query for related route/model metadata), i.e., avoid N+1.
  - Use pre-joined or denormalized summary fields for the list view.
- Required indexes (high level):
  - (org_id, project_id, suggestion_type, sort_key, suggestion_id)
  - (org_id, project_id, route_id)
  - (org_id, project_id, model)
  - (org_id, project_id, region)
- Store “summary” vs “details” separately if needed to keep list thin.

Response shaping (keep the list light)
List response should include only what the table needs:
- suggestion_id (stable)
- suggestion_type (batching|caching)
- target: route_id + route_name (or route display label), model, region/environment (as applicable)
- estimated_savings_usd_per_day (and percent)
- estimated_latency_delta_ms_p50 / p95 (or a compact representation)
- risk_level (Low/Medium/High)
- confidence (e.g., High/Medium/Low or numeric band) — keep small
- stale_as_of (top-level)
- “why_short” (1 sentence max) optional; longer copy belongs in details

Details response can include:
- why_long (bulleted reasons)
- assumptions (tokens priced at X, cache hit uplift assumptions, etc.)
- guardrails / “when not to apply”
- deep links to config pages

Error/timeout budgets
- Gateway timeout: 2s (hard)
- Service internal timeout for DB: 400ms (list), 300ms (details)
- Service internal timeout for cache: 30ms
- Retries:
  - No client retries on 4xx.
  - At most 1 retry on idempotent 5xx at gateway, with jitter; avoid thundering herd.

Availability targets (beta)
- Service SLO (beta): 99.5% successful responses (2xx/3xx) over 7 days.
- Error budget: 0.5% (note: beta; revisit for GA).

Observability requirements (for enforcing budget)
Golden signals (per endpoint)
- request rate (RPS), error rate (4xx/5xx), p50/p95/p99 latency
- cache hit rate (service cache), cache latency, cache errors
- DB query time distribution + query count per request (detect N+1)
- payload size distribution (gzip + raw)
- top offenders by org_id (rate + latency)

Dashboards/alerts (minimum)
- Alert: p95 list latency > 300ms for 10m (burn rate style)
- Alert: 5xx rate > 1% for 5m
- Alert: cache hit rate drops below 60% (investigate TTL/invalidation)
- Alert: compute lag > 2x cadence (data freshness)

Security/RBAC considerations that affect caching
- Cache must not “bleed” across users if RBAC differs. Options:
  A) include rbac_scope_hash in cache key (preferred)
  B) only cache org-level if all users share identical view permissions (not true)
- Safer approach for beta: cache at org+project level AND require permission to “view optimize suggestions” at that scope; still, users may have project exclusions, so hash is needed if we support mixed access.

Proposed rbac_scope_hash inputs
- user_id + org_id + list of allowed project_ids (or a role id)
- permission: optimize:suggestions:read
- NOTE: if this becomes too low hit-rate, revisit RBAC model to make permissions less granular for beta.

Load shedding / degradation behavior
- If DB is slow or cache is down:
  - Prefer serving stale cached responses if within TTL-grace (e.g., allow 5m stale-while-revalidate).
  - If neither available, return 503 with a specific error code that Console maps to a friendly error state.
- If payload too large (unexpected explanation bloats):
  - Truncate server-side with a “truncated: true” flag (string field in response) OR move fields behind details endpoint only.

Open questions / TODOs
- Do we need /optimize/suggestions/summary endpoint for “top opportunities” card elsewhere in Console? If yes, define separate budget.
- Should list endpoint include confidence bands (min/max savings) in beta? If yes, measure payload impact.
- Confirm default TTLs with Console team to avoid confusing “why did it change” in UI while user is browsing.
- Decide whether details endpoint is required for beta launch or can be deferred.

Revision history
- 2026-02-07: initial draft (Logan)
- 2026-02-12: tightened p95 ceilings; added payload budgets after early UI perf testing (Chloe/Felix)
- 2026-02-19: added RBAC cache key notes + stale-while-revalidate recommendation (Allison/Aisha)

- Safer approach for beta: cache at org+project level AND require permission to “view optimize suggestions” at that scope; still, users may have project exclusions, so hash is needed if we support mixed access.

Proposed rbac_scope_hash inputs
- user_id + org_id + list of allowed project_ids (or a role id)
- permission: optimize:suggestions:read
- NOTE: if this becomes too low hit-rate, revisit RBAC model to make permissions less granular for beta.

Load shedding / degradation behavior
- If DB is slow or cache is down:
  - Prefer serving stale cached responses if within TTL-grace (e.g., allow 5m stale-while-revalidate).
  - If neither available, return 503 with a specific error code that Console maps to a friendly error state.
- If payload too large (unexpected explanation bloats):
  - Truncate server-side with a “truncated: true” flag (string field in response) OR move fields behind details endpoint only.

Open questions / TODOs
- Do we need /optimize/suggestions/summary endpoint for “top opportunities” card elsewhere in Console? If yes, define separate budget.
- Should list endpoint include confidence bands (min/max savings) in beta? If yes, measure payload impact.
- Confirm default TTLs with Console team to avoid confusing “why did it change” in UI while user is browsing.
- Decide whether details endpoint is required for beta launch or can be deferred.

Revision history
- 2026-02-07: initial draft (Logan)
- 2026-02-12: tightened p95 ceilings; added payload budgets after early UI perf testing (Chloe/Felix)
- 2026-02-19: added RBAC cache key notes + stale-while-revalidate recommendation (Allison/Aisha)
text:
Doc intent
- Capture performance + scale budgets for the Console “Optimization suggestions” beta API.
- This is a constraints doc for engineering: if we can’t meet these numbers, Console UX (table + drawer) will feel sluggish and we’ll get pageload regressions.

Scope
- Suggestions list endpoint + (optional) details endpoint.
- Beta scope = batching + caching suggestions only.
- Org/project scoped, RBAC enforced. No cross-tenant access.

Non-goals
- Real-time per-request inference; suggestions are batch computed and served from storage.
- Guaranteeing “savings accuracy” (handled separately); this doc is about runtime performance and availability.

Assumptions / traffic model (beta)
- Primary UI path: Console loads Suggestions view once per user session; user may sort/filter/paginate; opens 0–5 drawers.
- Expected “active beta orgs”: 10–30 orgs initially, scaling to 100–200 orgs during expanded beta.
- Concurrent interactive users: 1–10 per org during working hours (spiky), with occasional demos.
- Background job compute cadence: hourly default (can be daily if compute pressure), but serving layer must tolerate stale data.

API endpoints (working contract)
- GET /optimize/suggestions
  - Query params: org_id, project_id (optional), environment (optional), route_id (optional), model (optional), region (optional), suggestion_type (batching|caching), sort, order, page_size, cursor
  - Returns: list of suggestion summaries, next_cursor, stale_as_of, request_id
- GET /optimize/suggestions/{suggestion_id}
  - Returns: the full details payload used by the drawer (why/explanation, assumptions, guardrails, links)

NOTE: If details endpoint is too expensive, we can embed “drawer-lite” content in list response for top N, but that likely increases payload size and hurts perceived speed on the main view.

Performance targets (server-side, excluding client rendering)
Table legend:
- p50/p95 measured at API gateway for 200 responses
- payload size is gzip’ed (over-the-wire) unless stated otherwise
- “cold” = cache miss at service cache layer; “warm” = cache hit

Table: latency + payload budgets
| Surface | Scenario | Target p50 | Target p95 | Hard ceiling p95 | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| GET /optimize/suggestions (list) | warm cache | 80 ms | 180 ms | 300 ms | Primary table load + filter/sort changes |
| GET /optimize/suggestions (list) | cold cache | 140 ms | 300 ms | 450 ms | Cold allowed but should be rare in interactive paths |
| GET /optimize/suggestions/{id} (details) | warm cache | 60 ms | 150 ms | 250 ms | Drawer open should feel instant |
| GET /optimize/suggestions/{id} (details) | cold cache | 120 ms | 250 ms | 400 ms | Drawer tolerates slightly higher; still keep tight |

Table: payload budgets
| Response | Target (gzip) | Hard ceiling (gzip) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| list page (<= 50 rows) | <= 35 KB | 60 KB | Prefer page_size default 25; 50 max |
| details payload | <= 20 KB | 40 KB | Avoid giant “explanation” blobs; link to docs when needed |
| error payload | <= 2 KB | 5 KB | Keep errors structured, don’t dump stack traces |

Page size / pagination budget
- Default page_size: 25
- Max page_size: 50 (server-enforced)
- Cursor-based pagination required for stable ordering + performance at scale.
- Sorting should be backed by indexed columns; forbid unindexed sorts.

Rate limits (beta)
Rationale: prevent accidental UI loops / eager refresh from hammering backend; keep headroom for compute pipeline + other Optimize traffic.

Table: rate limits
| Principal | Endpoint | Limit | Burst | Window | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| per-user | list | 60 req/min | 20 | 60s | Filter/sort + pagination should be within this |
| per-user | details | 120 req/min | 40 | 60s | Drawer opens can be chatty; still bounded |
| per-org | list | 600 req/min | 200 | 60s | Demos + multiple users |
| per-org | details | 1200 req/min | 400 | 60s | |
| per-IP (fallback) | all | 3000 req/5min | 500 | 300s | DDoS-ish guardrail |

Client guidance to reduce request volume
- UI should debounce filter changes (>= 250–400ms).
- Use “Apply filters” button if we see over-fetching.
- Cache list pages client-side for 30–60s keyed by (org, project, filters, sort, cursor).

Caching strategy
Goals:
- Keep list endpoint p95 < 300ms reliably.
- Avoid repeated DB hits for identical list queries during active usage.
- Support multi-tenant correctness (cache keys must include org_id + project scope + RBAC context).

Proposed layers
1) CDN / edge caching: NOT recommended for beta
- Reason: RBAC + org scoped data; cache fragmentation and security risk.

2) Service-level cache (recommended)
- Cache store: Redis (or equivalent) in-region with the service.
- Cache key inputs (must include): org_id, project_id (or “all”), environment, route_id, model, region, suggestion_type, sort, order, cursor/page boundary, page_size, and a “rbac_scope_hash”.
- TTL:
  - list pages: 60s default (tunable 30–180s)
  - details: 5m default (tunable 2m–15m)
- Invalidation:
  - Passive TTL is fine for beta.
  - Optional: invalidate org keys when new suggestion batch is published (publish event emits org_id + generation timestamp).

3) DB query caching (not primary)
- Avoid relying on DB-level cache; we want deterministic performance with predictable memory pressure.

Staleness behavior
- API must return: stale_as_of timestamp for the dataset used to generate suggestions.
- Console should show “Updated X hours ago” and avoid auto-refresh loops.
- If stale_as_of > 48h (configurable), show warning state but still serve data.

DB / storage performance requirements
- The list endpoint must be implementable as:
  - 1 query for suggestions page (plus possibly 1 query for related route/model metadata), i.e., avoid N+1.
  - Use pre-joined or denormalized summary fields for the list view.
- Required indexes (high level):
  - (org_id, project_id, suggestion_type, sort_key, suggestion_id)
  - (org_id, project_id, route_id)
  - (org_id, project_id, model)
  - (org_id, project_id, region)
- Store “summary” vs “details” separately if needed to keep list thin.

Response shaping (keep the list light)
List response should include only what the table needs:
- suggestion_id (stable)
- suggestion_type (batching|caching)
- target: route_id + route_name (or route display label), model, region/environment (as applicable)
- estimated_savings_usd_per_day (and percent)
- estimated_latency_delta_ms_p50 / p95 (or a compact representation)
- risk_level (Low/Medium/High)
- confidence (e.g., High/Medium/Low or numeric band) — keep small
- stale_as_of (top-level)
- “why_short” (1 sentence max) optional; longer copy belongs in details

Details response can include:
- why_long (bulleted reasons)
- assumptions (tokens priced at X, cache hit uplift assumptions, etc.)
- guardrails / “when not to apply”
- deep links to config pages

Error/timeout budgets
- Gateway timeout: 2s (hard)
- Service internal timeout for DB: 400ms (list), 300ms (details)
- Service internal timeout for cache: 30ms
- Retries:
  - No client retries on 4xx.
  - At most 1 retry on idempotent 5xx at gateway, with jitter; avoid thundering herd.

Availability targets (beta)
- Service SLO (beta): 99.5% successful responses (2xx/3xx) over 7 days.
- Error budget: 0.5% (note: beta; revisit for GA).

Observability requirements (for enforcing budget)
Golden signals (per endpoint)
- request rate (RPS), error rate (4xx/5xx), p50/p95/p99 latency
- cache hit rate (service cache), cache latency, cache errors
- DB query time distribution + query count per request (detect N+1)
- payload size distribution (gzip + raw)
- top offenders by org_id (rate + latency)

Dashboards/alerts (minimum)
- Alert: p95 list latency > 300ms for 10m (burn rate style)
- Alert: 5xx rate > 1% for 5m
- Alert: cache hit rate drops below 60% (investigate TTL/invalidation)
- Alert: compute lag > 2x cadence (data freshness)

Security/RBAC considerations that affect caching
- Cache must not “bleed” across users if RBAC differs. Options:
  A) include rbac_scope_hash in cache key (preferred)
  B) only cache org-level if all users share identical view permissions (not true)
- Safer approach for beta: cache at org+project level AND require permission to “view optimize suggestions” at that scope; still, users may have project exclusions, so hash is needed if we support mixed access.

Proposed rbac_scope_hash inputs
- user_id + org_id + list of allowed project_ids (or a role id)
- permission: optimize:suggestions:read
- NOTE: if this becomes too low hit-rate, revisit RBAC model to make permissions less granular for beta.

Load shedding / degradation behavior
- If DB is slow or cache is down:
  - Prefer serving stale cached responses if within TTL-grace (e.g., allow 5m stale-while-revalidate).
  - If neither available, return 503 with a specific error code that Console maps to a friendly error state.
- If payload too large (unexpected explanation bloats):
  - Truncate server-side with a “truncated: true” flag (string field in response) OR move fields behind details endpoint only.

Open questions / TODOs
- Do we need /optimize/suggestions/summary endpoint for “top opportunities” card elsewhere in Console? If yes, define separate budget.
- Should list endpoint include confidence bands (min/max savings) in beta? If yes, measure payload impact.
- Confirm default TTLs with Console team to avoid confusing “why did it change” in UI while user is browsing.
- Decide whether details endpoint is required for beta launch or can be deferred.

Revision history
- 2026-02-07: initial draft (Logan)
- 2026-02-12: tightened p95 ceilings; added payload budgets after early UI perf testing (Chloe/Felix)
- 2026-02-19: added RBAC cache key notes + stale-while-revalidate recommendation (Allison/Aisha)

source.title: Suggestions (Batching/Caching) — Data Requirements (v0, beta)
source.content:
Context
- This doc enumerates the minimum telemetry signals required to generate Optimization Suggestions (beta scope: batching + caching only) with (a) estimated cost savings ($/day, %), (b) estimated performance impact (p50/p95 latency deltas), and (c) risk label inputs (Low/Medium/High) that are explainable.
- Audience: Telemetry/Observability + Optimize pipeline engineers + Console backend.
- Output consumer: Optimize Suggestions Service compute job + Suggestions API + Console UI.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- UI funnel events (impression/expand/dismiss/apply). Those are specified elsewhere (see ENG-19402).
- Full “quality regression” eval data (prompt sets, output scoring). We only need enough proxy signals for performance/reliability risk in v0.

Key definitions (align on naming)
- Org / Project: tenant and sub-tenant boundary. All aggregates MUST respect these boundaries.
- Route: request classification in Redwood Gateway (e.g., /v1/chat.completions, /v1/embeddings) plus customer-defined route labels if enabled.
- Model: canonical model identifier and version (e.g., llama-3.1-70b-instruct@2026-01-15).
- Deployment mode: hosted | dedicated | private (VPC). (For beta we expect hosted + dedicated first; private can be read-only if telemetry differs.)
- Region: cloud region / POP (used for capacity + latency analysis).
- Suggestion unit (v0): keyed by (org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region) with rollup options at route+model across regions.

Eligibility gates (data must exist before we show suggestions)
1) Minimum traffic volume
   - Recommend: >= 10,000 requests/day OR >= 25M input_tokens/day (whichever hits first) per (route, model) to keep estimator stable.
   - If below threshold, show “Not enough traffic to estimate reliably” (Console uses this as an empty state reason).
2) Freshness
   - Aggregates no older than 2 hours for “current” view. If older, we can still render but must mark as stale (stale_as_of).
3) Coverage
   - At least 90% of requests must have (a) latency, (b) token counts, and (c) cache/batching metrics (when relevant) populated.

Required signal categories
A) Traffic + cost attribution (required for $ savings)
B) Latency distribution (required for performance impact + risk inputs)
C) Cache metrics (required for caching suggestions)
D) Batching metrics (required for batching suggestions)
E) Reliability / saturation proxies (required for risk inputs)
F) Config + capability metadata (required to determine what’s changeable + guardrails)

A) Traffic + cost attribution signals
Purpose
- Compute baseline spend by route/model and translate estimated token savings or compute-time savings into $/day.

Required fields (per aggregate window)
- request_count
- input_tokens_sum
- output_tokens_sum
- total_tokens_sum (or compute from above)
- billable_tokens_sum (if differs; prefer explicit)
- effective_unit_price_usd_per_1k_tokens (or joinable pricing key)
- cost_usd_sum (preferred if cost attribution pipeline already produces it; otherwise compute in estimator)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id
- route_id
- model_id (with version)
- region
- deployment_mode

Aggregation
- Primary rollup: 5-minute buckets for last 24h, plus 1-hour buckets for 14 days.
- Estimator uses: last 7 days default window; fallback to last 24h if limited history.

Notes
- Pricing can change; do not bake static token price into the estimator. Either:
  (1) store cost_usd_sum already computed by cost attribution (preferred), OR
  (2) store a join key (pricing_plan_id + effective rate) versioned by time.

B) Latency distribution signals
Purpose
- Estimate impact of enabling batching/caching on p50/p95, and generate risk-level inputs.

Required fields
- latency_ms_histogram (preferred) OR p50_ms/p90_ms/p95_ms + count
- queue_time_ms_histogram (if available) OR p95_queue_time_ms
- ttft_ms_histogram (time-to-first-token) for streaming routes (important for perceived latency)
- generation_time_ms_histogram (optional but helpful)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode
- request_class: streaming vs non-streaming (boolean is fine)

Aggregation
- 5-minute buckets for last 24h
- 1-hour buckets for 14 days
- For histogram: standard fixed buckets shared across services (see Allison’s metrics standards).

Data quality requirements
- Latency must be end-to-end user-visible latency measured at the Gateway (not just model runtime). If only runtime is available, we need both.
- Histograms must include count and sum (or explicit pXX + count) to allow confidence computation.

C) Cache metrics (prefix/KV cache)
Purpose
- Determine current caching effectiveness and estimate savings from increasing cache hits.

Required fields
- cache_eligible_request_count
- cache_hit_request_count
- cache_miss_request_count
- cache_hit_rate (can compute)
- cache_saved_input_tokens_sum (preferred) OR cache_hit_input_tokens_sum
- cache_lookup_latency_ms_p95 (optional; helps risk)
- cache_evictions_count (optional)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode
- cache_scope: per-route vs global (string)

Important clarifications
- We need “eligible” vs “hit” to avoid misleading hit rate.
- If cache metrics are emitted at runtime and not at Gateway, ensure correlation keys allow rollup to the same route/model buckets.

Aggregation + retention
- Same as A/B.
- Additionally, keep 30-day daily rollups for cache_hit_rate trend (used for “confidence” and seasonality checks).

D) Batching metrics
Purpose
- Estimate whether continuous batching can increase throughput (reduce $/token) without violating latency SLOs.

Required fields
- concurrency_avg (in-flight requests average)
- concurrency_p95 (or histogram)
- inter_arrival_time_ms_p50 (optional, derived from request rate)
- batch_size_avg (actual observed micro-batch size) OR tokens_per_batch_avg
- batch_wait_ms_p50/p95 (time waiting to form batch) (critical for latency deltas)
- scheduler_queue_depth_p95 (or equivalent)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Notes
- If batch_size is not observable in hosted mode (engine abstraction), we can approximate from runtime metrics (kernel-level) but MUST document assumptions in estimator.
- For beta, we can start with concurrency + queue_time proxies and add true batch metrics later, but then risk scoring must be conservative.

E) Reliability / saturation proxies (risk inputs)
Purpose
- Risk label should reflect likelihood of latency regression or increased error rate when enabling caching/batching.

Required fields
- error_rate_5xx (Gateway)
- timeout_rate
- rate_limit_dropped_count (if applicable)
- retries_count (if client retries are visible; otherwise server-side retry)
- gpu_utilization_avg (dedicated only; hosted optional)
- memory_pressure_events_count (OOM, eviction spikes) (if available)
- p95_queue_time_ms (also listed in B; explicitly required for risk)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Interpretation
- These are NOT “security risk” signals; they feed performance/reliability risk only.

F) Config + capability metadata
Purpose
- Suggestions must know what is currently enabled and what levers are available, and provide safe guardrails.

Required fields (snapshot, not timeseries)
- current_batching_enabled (bool)
- current_batching_max_batch_size (int)
- current_batching_max_wait_ms (int)
- current_cache_enabled (bool)
- current_cache_scope (string)
- current_cache_ttl_seconds (int)
- route_latency_slo_p95_ms (optional; if customer set)
- route_priority (interactive vs background) (optional but useful)
- model_supports_prefix_cache (bool)
- model_supports_continuous_batching (bool)
- deployment_capacity_tier (hosted tier / dedicated pool id)

Dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Source of truth
- Console config service / rollouts config store. Do NOT scrape from UI state.

Retention windows (proposal)
We need three tiers: raw logs, high-resolution aggregates, and long-term rollups.

1) Raw request logs (for backfill + audits)
- Retention: 14 days (minimum), target 30 days if storage allows.
- Contains: request_id, org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, timestamps, latency_ms, tokens in/out, status code, streaming flag.
- Privacy: no prompts/completions in this pipeline. Tokens counts are OK.

2) High-resolution aggregates (for estimator + UI)
- 5-minute buckets: retention 14 days
- 1-hour buckets: retention 90 days
- Used by: Suggestions compute job (hourly/daily) and UI “recent baseline” display.

3) Long-term rollups (for trend + seasonality)
- Daily buckets: retention 13 months
- Used by: confidence heuristics (weekday patterns), support investigations (“why did estimate change?”).

Backfill feasibility
Goal
- When enabling beta for an org, we want suggestions to appear quickly (same day) if we have usable historical telemetry.

Backfill sources
- Primary: raw request logs + existing cost attribution aggregates (if already stored by org/project/route/model).
- Secondary: tracing spans (if request logs missing). Tracing is often sampled; treat as partial coverage and mark confidence low.

Backfill strategy (recommended)
- Backfill 7 days of 1-hour aggregates + 24h of 5-minute aggregates.
- Compute job can run a “catch-up” mode when feature flag is enabled.

Backfill limitations
- Cache/batching metrics may not exist historically (depending on when runtime started emitting them). If missing:
  - Show caching/batching suggestions only when the relevant metrics have >= 72 hours coverage.
  - Otherwise, show “Insufficient telemetry for caching/batching estimates” and link to setup docs.
- Pricing joins: if pricing plan history is missing, fall back to cost_usd_sum if available; if neither, show % savings only (no $) and clearly label.

Data contract notes (cardinality + performance)
- The compute job and Suggestions API need stable, bounded cardinality. Route_id should be normalized (avoid raw URL paths with IDs).
- Expected top cardinality keys per org:
  - route_id: <= 200
  - model_id: <= 50
  - region: <= 10
  - total suggestion units: <= 200 * 50 * 10 = 100k worst-case; in practice much lower.
- If org exceeds thresholds, we should:
  - roll up to top-N by cost, and
  - include “Other” bucket for cost totals, but do not generate suggestions for it.

Freshness + staleness behavior
- Suggestions results include stale_as_of. This must map to the newest aggregate timestamp used by the estimator.
- If staleness > 6 hours:
  - still show suggestions list but label “Data delayed” and reduce confidence.
- If staleness > 24 hours:
  - treat as degraded; optionally suppress new suggestions and show warning.

Data validation checks (to run in pipeline)
- Token sanity: input_tokens_sum + output_tokens_sum ~= total_tokens_sum within tolerance.
- Cost sanity: cost_usd_sum >= 0; price within expected bounds per plan.
- Latency sanity: p95 >= p50; histograms non-empty when request_count > 0.
- Coverage: % requests with tokens and latency; emit a “telemetry_coverage_ratio” per unit.
- Cache sanity: hit_count <= eligible_count; hit_rate in [0,1].

Open questions / TODO
1) Do we have a consistent definition of route_id across Hosted vs Dedicated gateways? If not, we need a normalization layer.
2) For dedicated customers, can we reliably attribute GPU utilization to (route, model) without leaking cross-project info?
3) Should we store TTFT for non-streaming routes (meaningless) or keep nulls? Preference: keep field but only populate for streaming.
4) Confirm retention feasibility with Nadia (cost attribution storage) + Caleb (trace/log storage).

Appendix: Minimal required fields checklist (beta)
- request_count
- input_tokens_sum, output_tokens_sum
- cost_usd_sum OR effective_unit_price join key
- latency_ms p50/p95 (or histogram)
- queue_time_ms p95 (or histogram)
- cache_eligible_request_count + cache_hit_request_count (for caching suggestions)
- concurrency_avg/p95 (for batching suggestions)
- error_rate_5xx + timeout_rate
- config snapshot: batching enabled + params, caching enabled + scope/ttl

Owner notes (Logan)
- If we’re forced to cut scope for beta, do NOT cut: cost_usd_sum (or equivalent) + p95 latency + queue_time. Without those, savings and risk become too hand-wavy and we’ll lose trust fast.

C) Cache metrics (prefix/KV cache)
Purpose
- Determine current caching effectiveness and estimate savings from increasing cache hits.

Required fields
- cache_eligible_request_count
- cache_hit_request_count
- cache_miss_request_count
- cache_hit_rate (can compute)
- cache_saved_input_tokens_sum (preferred) OR cache_hit_input_tokens_sum
- cache_lookup_latency_ms_p95 (optional; helps risk)
- cache_evictions_count (optional)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode
- cache_scope: per-route vs global (string)

Important clarifications
- We need “eligible” vs “hit” to avoid misleading hit rate.
- If cache metrics are emitted at runtime and not at Gateway, ensure correlation keys allow rollup to the same route/model buckets.

Aggregation + retention
- Same as A/B.
- Additionally, keep 30-day daily rollups for cache_hit_rate trend (used for “confidence” and seasonality checks).

D) Batching metrics
Purpose
- Estimate whether continuous batching can increase throughput (reduce $/token) without violating latency SLOs.

Required fields
- concurrency_avg (in-flight requests average)
- concurrency_p95 (or histogram)
- inter_arrival_time_ms_p50 (optional, derived from request rate)
- batch_size_avg (actual observed micro-batch size) OR tokens_per_batch_avg
- batch_wait_ms_p50/p95 (time waiting to form batch) (critical for latency deltas)
- scheduler_queue_depth_p95 (or equivalent)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Notes
- If batch_size is not observable in hosted mode (engine abstraction), we can approximate from runtime metrics (kernel-level) but MUST document assumptions in estimator.
- For beta, we can start with concurrency + queue_time proxies and add true batch metrics later, but then risk scoring must be conservative.

E) Reliability / saturation proxies (risk inputs)
Purpose
- Risk label should reflect likelihood of latency regression or increased error rate when enabling caching/batching.

Required fields
- error_rate_5xx (Gateway)
- timeout_rate
- rate_limit_dropped_count (if applicable)
- retries_count (if client retries are visible; otherwise server-side retry)
- gpu_utilization_avg (dedicated only; hosted optional)
- memory_pressure_events_count (OOM, eviction spikes) (if available)
- p95_queue_time_ms (also listed in B; explicitly required for risk)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Interpretation
- These are NOT “security risk” signals; they feed performance/reliability risk only.

F) Config + capability metadata
Purpose
- Suggestions must know what is currently enabled and what levers are available, and provide safe guardrails.

Required fields (snapshot, not timeseries)
- current_batching_enabled (bool)
- current_batching_max_batch_size (int)
- current_batching_max_wait_ms (int)
- current_cache_enabled (bool)
- current_cache_scope (string)
- current_cache_ttl_seconds (int)
- route_latency_slo_p95_ms (optional; if customer set)
- route_priority (interactive vs background) (optional but useful)
- model_supports_prefix_cache (bool)
- model_supports_continuous_batching (bool)
- deployment_capacity_tier (hosted tier / dedicated pool id)

Dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

Source of truth
- Console config service / rollouts config store. Do NOT scrape from UI state.

Retention windows (proposal)
We need three tiers: raw logs, high-resolution aggregates, and long-term rollups.

1) Raw request logs (for backfill + audits)
- Retention: 14 days (minimum), target 30 days if storage allows.
- Contains: request_id, org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, timestamps, latency_ms, tokens in/out, status code, streaming flag.
- Privacy: no prompts/completions in this pipeline. Tokens counts are OK.

2) High-resolution aggregates (for estimator + UI)
- 5-minute buckets: retention 14 days
- 1-hour buckets: retention 90 days
- Used by: Suggestions compute job (hourly/daily) and UI “recent baseline” display.

3) Long-term rollups (for trend + seasonality)
- Daily buckets: retention 13 months
- Used by: confidence heuristics (weekday patterns), support investigations (“why did estimate change?”).

Backfill feasibility
Goal
- When enabling beta for an org, we want suggestions to appear quickly (same day) if we have usable historical telemetry.

Backfill sources
- Primary: raw request logs + existing cost attribution aggregates (if already stored by org/project/route/model).
- Secondary: tracing spans (if request logs missing). Tracing is often sampled; treat as partial coverage and mark confidence low.

Backfill strategy (recommended)
- Backfill 7 days of 1-hour aggregates + 24h of 5-minute aggregates.
- Compute job can run a “catch-up” mode when feature flag is enabled.

Backfill limitations
- Cache/batching metrics may not exist historically (depending on when runtime started emitting them). If missing:
  - Show caching/batching suggestions only when the relevant metrics have >= 72 hours coverage.
  - Otherwise, show “Insufficient telemetry for caching/batching estimates” and link to setup docs.
- Pricing joins: if pricing plan history is missing, fall back to cost_usd_sum if available; if neither, show % savings only (no $) and clearly label.

Data contract notes (cardinality + performance)
- The compute job and Suggestions API need stable, bounded cardinality. Route_id should be normalized (avoid raw URL paths with IDs).
- Expected top cardinality keys per org:
  - route_id: <= 200
  - model_id: <= 50
  - region: <= 10
  - total suggestion units: <= 200 * 50 * 10 = 100k worst-case; in practice much lower.
- If org exceeds thresholds, we should:
  - roll up to top-N by cost, and
  - include “Other” bucket for cost totals, but do not generate suggestions for it.

Freshness + staleness behavior
- Suggestions results include stale_as_of. This must map to the newest aggregate timestamp used by the estimator.
- If staleness > 6 hours:
  - still show suggestions list but label “Data delayed” and reduce confidence.
- If staleness > 24 hours:
  - treat as degraded; optionally suppress new suggestions and show warning.

Data validation checks (to run in pipeline)
- Token sanity: input_tokens_sum + output_tokens_sum ~= total_tokens_sum within tolerance.
- Cost sanity: cost_usd_sum >= 0; price within expected bounds per plan.
- Latency sanity: p95 >= p50; histograms non-empty when request_count > 0.
- Coverage: % requests with tokens and latency; emit a “telemetry_coverage_ratio” per unit.
- Cache sanity: hit_count <= eligible_count; hit_rate in [0,1].

Open questions / TODO
1) Do we have a consistent definition of route_id across Hosted vs Dedicated gateways? If not, we need a normalization layer.
2) For dedicated customers, can we reliably attribute GPU utilization to (route, model) without leaking cross-project info?
3) Should we store TTFT for non-streaming routes (meaningless) or keep nulls? Preference: keep field but only populate for streaming.
4) Confirm retention feasibility with Nadia (cost attribution storage) + Caleb (trace/log storage).

Appendix: Minimal required fields checklist (beta)
- request_count
- input_tokens_sum, output_tokens_sum
- cost_usd_sum OR effective_unit_price join key
- latency_ms p50/p95 (or histogram)
- queue_time_ms p95 (or histogram)
- cache_eligible_request_count + cache_hit_request_count (for caching suggestions)
- concurrency_avg/p95 (for batching suggestions)
- error_rate_5xx + timeout_rate
- config snapshot: batching enabled + params, caching enabled + scope/ttl

Owner notes (Logan)
- If we’re forced to cut scope for beta, do NOT cut: cost_usd_sum (or equivalent) + p95 latency + queue_time. Without those, savings and risk become too hand-wavy and we’ll lose trust fast.

- Audience: Telemetry/Observability + Optimize pipeline engineers + Console backend.
- Output consumer: Optimize Suggestions Service compute job + Suggestions API + Console UI.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- UI funnel events (impression/expand/dismiss/apply). Those are specified elsewhere (see ENG-19402).

Key definitions (align on naming)
- Org / Project: tenant and sub-tenant boundary. All aggregates MUST respect these boundaries.
- Route: request classification in Redwood Gateway (e.g., /v1/chat.completions, /v1/embeddings) plus customer-defined route labels if enabled.
- Model: canonical model identifier and version (e.g., llama-3.1-70b-instruct@2026-01-15).
- Deployment mode: hosted | dedicated | private (VPC). (For beta we expect hosted + dedicated first; private can be read-only if telemetry differs.)
- Region: cloud region / POP (used for capacity + latency analysis).

Eligibility gates (data must exist before we show suggestions)

2) Freshness
   - Aggregates no older than 2 hours for “current” view. If older, we can still render but must mark as stale (stale_as_of).
3) Coverage

B) Latency distribution signals

D) Batching metrics
Purpose

- concurrency_p95 (or histogram)
- inter_arrival_time_ms_p50 (optional, derived from request rate)
- batch_size_avg (actual observed micro-batch size) OR tokens_per_batch_avg
- batch_wait_ms_p50/p95 (time waiting to form batch) (critical for latency deltas)
- scheduler_queue_depth_p95 (or equivalent)

Required dimensions
- org_id, project_id, route_id, model_id, region, deployment_mode

- For beta, we can start with concurrency + queue_time proxies and add true batch metrics later, but then risk scoring must be conservative.

E) Reliability / saturation proxies (risk inputs)
Purpose
- Risk label should reflect likelihood of latency regression or increased error rate when enabling caching/batching.

Required fields

- timeout_rate

Required dimensions

Interpretation

F) Config + capability metadata
Purpose

Required fields (snapshot, not timeseries)
- current_batching_enabled (bool)
- current_batching_max_batch_size (int)

Retention windows (proposal)
We need three tiers: raw logs, high-resolution aggregates, and long-term rollups.

source.title: Tier tagging + metrics propagation plan (Hosted vs Dedicated) — SLO/Error Budget program
source.content:
Context
- We’re rolling out tiered SLOs + error budgets for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). For those to be credible, tier labeling must be:
  (1) set once (authoritative),
  (2) propagated end-to-end across gateway → routing/control plane → model serving,
  (3) present on the right metrics/logs/traces used for SLIs, and
  (4) safe from cardinality blow-ups.
- This doc is the concrete implementation plan for “tier tagging” in telemetry. It’s intended as the working plan used by Telemetry + Gateway + Routing to land ENG-4922 and unblock dashboards/alerts.

Goals
1) Canonical tags: define the minimal label set required to compute SLIs for availability, latency, and streaming reliability by tier/region/endpoint class.
2) Authoritative source: tier tag set at ingress (API gateway), not “inferred later” in routing.
3) Propagation: tier tag carried in request context and applied consistently to:
   - metrics emitted at gateway
   - metrics emitted in routing/control plane
   - metrics emitted in model serving request path
   - distributed traces and structured logs (for debug/attribution)
4) Cardinality guardrails: avoid per-customer/per-model/per-request labeling on high-volume metrics. Provide a controlled path for Dedicated account-level SLO dashboards.
5) Validation and rollout: tier tag coverage checks (per endpoint/tier/region), staged rollout (shadow labels → dashboards → alerting → enforcement).

Non-goals (for this phase)
- Private deployment tier tagging (we will keep a placeholder label value; enforcement is not in scope this quarter).
- Full per-customer SLO reporting for Hosted (we will not do account_id tags on high-volume Hosted metrics).

Canonical taxonomy (what tags we will use)
Required tags (must exist on all SLI-bearing metrics)
- deployment_tier: one of {hosted, dedicated, private_unknown}
  - Notes: “private_unknown” is a placeholder for future; do NOT treat it as a tier with SLO enforcement.
- region: cloud region identifier (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1)
- endpoint_class: one of {chat, completions, embeddings, rerank}
  - Notes: keep this coarse to limit series count.
- transport: one of {http, sse}
  - sse used for streaming/chat stream where disconnect accounting matters.

Recommended tags (allowed where series volume is bounded)
- route_group: stable, low-cardinality logical route (e.g., “v1_chat_completions”, “v1_embeddings”). Do not use raw URL path.
- model_family: coarse bucket (e.g., “llama3”, “mixtral”, “qwen2”) rather than model_id.
- status_class: {2xx, 4xx, 5xx, timeout, cancel}
  - Note: status_class is not needed on latency histograms if we track success latency separately.

Strictly prohibited on high-volume metrics
- request_id, trace_id
- raw_model_id / model_version (unless pre-bucketed)
- customer_id / account_id for Hosted
- prompt tokens / completion tokens as labels (use exemplars or logs)

Dedicated account tagging (special case)
- dedicated_account_slug: allowed only on Dedicated SLO “thin” metrics with controlled sampling OR on derived/recording-rule metrics created from low-cardinality source series.
  - We will not add dedicated_account_slug to gateway request counters by default.
  - Proposed compromise:
    - Base SLIs: aggregate by deployment_tier only.
    - Dedicated customer dashboards: computed via dedicated control-plane mapping + query-time filtering of dedicated clusters / namespaces, not per-request account_id labels.
  - If Product/Sales requires per-account SLO numbers, we should generate them as scheduled jobs (budget computation pipeline) rather than raw Prometheus series explosion.

Where tags are set (source of truth)
A) API Gateway (ingress) — authoritative tier assignment
- Set deployment_tier at the earliest point we can reliably identify:
  - Dedicated: request resolved to a Dedicated environment via auth/API key mapping (control plane lookup or cached map). Result must be deterministic.
  - Hosted: default for all other valid authenticated requests.
  - Invalid/unauthenticated requests: still labeled (deployment_tier=hosted) but excluded in SLI computation via SLI filters (see below).
- Region is known at gateway instance level.
- endpoint_class and transport derived from route handler and response mode (SSE vs HTTP).

B) Request context propagation
- The gateway attaches a “tier context” object to the internal request envelope forwarded to routing/control plane:
  - deployment_tier
  - tier_source: {auth_map, header_override, default}
  - dedicated_cluster_id (Dedicated only; internal id, not customer identifier)
- The context is propagated:
  - through routing policy evaluation
  - into model serving request metadata
  - into trace attributes
  - into log fields
- Key requirement: fallback/reroute must preserve original deployment_tier. (Routing may change model backend/region, but tier stays the customer contract tier.)

C) Routing/control plane
- Must *not* re-derive tier. Must accept tier from context and pass through.
- Emits routing decision metrics with deployment_tier and region:
  - route decision counts
  - fallback events
  - overload shed events

D) Model serving path
- Serving runtime emits request/latency metrics keyed by deployment_tier + region + endpoint_class + transport.
- Avoid per-model/per-replica labels. If we need “top contributors,” we should do it via logs + traces and sampled metrics.

How tags appear in telemetry
1) Metrics (Prometheus-style; naming illustrative)
Gateway
- redwood_api_requests_total{deployment_tier, region, endpoint_class, transport, status_class}
- redwood_api_request_duration_seconds_bucket{deployment_tier, region, endpoint_class, transport}
- redwood_stream_disconnects_total{deployment_tier, region, endpoint_class="chat", transport="sse", disconnect_reason}

Routing/control plane
- redwood_routing_decisions_total{deployment_tier, region, decision="primary|fallback|reroute"}
- redwood_overload_shed_total{deployment_tier, region, shed_reason}
- redwood_policy_eval_latency_seconds_bucket{deployment_tier, region}

Serving
- redwood_model_requests_total{deployment_tier, region, endpoint_class, status_class}
- redwood_model_request_duration_seconds_bucket{deployment_tier, region, endpoint_class}
- redwood_kv_cache_hit_total{deployment_tier, region}  (optional; keep low-cardinality)

2) Traces
- Trace root span (gateway) must include attributes:
  - deployment_tier
  - region
  - endpoint_class
  - transport
  - dedicated_cluster_id (Dedicated only; internal)
- Downstream spans should inherit deployment_tier and region automatically (propagation).

3) Logs
- Structured log fields on gateway + routing + serving:
  - deployment_tier
  - region
  - endpoint_class
  - transport
  - decision / fallback flags (routing)
- Avoid including account_id in logs unless already part of auth context and access controlled. If present, ensure it’s redacted/hashed per logging policy.

SLI accounting rules (how tags + filters combine)
Availability / success rate SLI
- Numerator: successful requests
- Denominator: valid requests
- Valid request definition (initial proposal; align with SLI spec doc):
  - Include: all requests reaching gateway handler with valid auth and passing basic request validation
  - Exclude: 4xx due to client errors (invalid request schema, unsupported params), rate limited (429), and explicit client aborts
  - Include in “error”: 5xx, gateway timeouts, upstream timeouts, serving errors, stream disconnects attributable to server

Latency SLI
- Compute latency histograms on successful requests only (to avoid “fast failures” hiding tail latency).
- Use end-to-end timing boundary:
  - Start: gateway request received
  - Stop:
    - non-streaming: response headers committed
    - streaming (SSE): time-to-first-token (TTFT) and stream duration tracked separately

Streaming reliability SLI
- Define stream failure as server-side disconnect/error prior to “normal completion.”
- Track reasons:
  - server_error
  - upstream_reset
  - gateway_timeout
  - client_abort (excluded from SLI)
- Ensure disconnect_reason is coarse and bounded.

Propagation edge cases (must be handled)
Retries
- Client retries: counted as separate requests (normal).
- Internal retries (gateway → routing or routing → serving):
  - Keep request_id constant in tracing only; do NOT emit extra request_total increments for internal retries unless explicitly part of SLI definition.
  - Emit separate retry metrics: redwood_internal_retries_total{deployment_tier, region, component}

Fallback/reroute
